title: Pixel Pangolins
short_description: Use the number clues on each row and column to work out which squares to fill — and a hidden pixel picture appears. Classic nonograms, kid-sized.
description: Every row and column has number clues telling you how many squares are filled and in what runs — put them together and a little pixel picture reveals itself, from hearts and arrows to houses and stars. It's pure deductive logic: cross-referencing the clues, marking squares you know must be blank, and reasoning your way to the only picture that fits. Unlike the sudoku game, these are picross/nonogram puzzles, so the payoff is a picture. Grade bands ramp from gentle 5×5 grids, to 6×6, to trickier 7×7 pictures. Solving without peeking and building streaks earns bonus points. A free online nonogram, picross and deductive-reasoning logic game for kids in grades 1–5, feeding your score and GP on OnlyGrins.
controls: Mouse / tap squares to fill them; switch to Mark mode to note blanks; then Check.
